text:
WIC: How to Apply | Sutter County, CA
Show/Hide
ROAD CLOSURES
COMMUNITY ACADEMY
Determining WIC Eligibility
Three guidelines
are used to determine if an applicant is eligible to participate in WIC:
Category:
An applicant must be in one of the categories served by WIC
Pregnant women (recommended to sign up in the first trimester of pregnancy)
Breastfeeding women (up to 1 year after delivery)
Non-breastfeeding women (up to 6 months after the pregnancy)
Infants
Children (under the age of 5)
Residence:
The applicant must live in California (preferably Sutter County)
Income:
The applicant must be within WIC guidelines (based on income before taxes)
If you are currently pregnant, count yourself as a household of two (2). Household means a group of people (related or not) who are living as one economic unit.
Click this
link
for the income eligibility guidelines.
How to Apply
A short screening process over the phone or in-person can help you find out if you qualify for the Sutter County WIC Program. You can call us at: (530) 822-7224 or stop by our main office at 1445 Veterans Memorial Circle in Yuba City.
You can also fill out our
online interest form
and we will reach out to you. Once the screening has been completed, you will be given an enrollment appointment at our main office. Appointments are about an hour long. Most participants are enrolled and
begin receiving benefits within two weeks
of the initial screening.
Last Updated December 29, 2025
